task:
Implement the following plan:

# Plan: Update READMEs with Full Script Inventory and Timing Data

## Context

The repository has grown from 16 to 30 scripts (14 new algorithms added). The project-level README and all three section-level READMEs still reference the original 16 scripts. The user wants all READMEs updated to reflect the full 30-script inventory, and wants section-level READMEs to include actual run timing captured via `time python <script>.py` executed sequentially.

## Step 1: Run All 30 Scripts Sequentially with Timing

Run each script one-at-a-time using `time python <script>.py` to capture wall-clock time. Execute in directory order within each tier. Capture real/user/sys times — use the `real` value for the README.

**Order:**
```
# 01-foundations (11 scripts)
time python 01-foundations/microbert.py
time python 01-foundations/microconv.py
time python 01-foundations/microdiffusion.py
time python 01-foundations/microembedding.py
time python 01-foundations/microgan.py
time python 01-foundations/microgpt.py
time python 01-foundations/microoptimizer.py
time python 01-foundations/microrag.py
time python 01-foundations/micrornn.py
time python 01-foundations/microtokenizer.py
time python 01-foundations/microvae.py

# 02-alignment (9 scripts)
time python 02-alignment/microbatchnorm.py
time python 02-alignment/microdpo.py
time python 02-alignment/microdropout.py
time python 02-alignment/microgrpo.py
time python 02-alignment/microlora.py
time python 02-alignment/micromoe.py
time python 02-alignment/microppo.py
time python 02-alignment/microqlora.py
time python 02-alignment/microreinforce.py

# 03-systems (10 scripts)
time python 03-systems/microattention.py
time python 03-systems/microbeam.py
time python 03-systems/microcheckpoint.py
time python 03-systems/microflash.py
time python 03-systems/microkv.py
time python 03-systems/micropaged.py
time python 03-systems/microparallel.py
time python 03-systems/microquant.py
time python 03-systems/microrope.py
time py

I am thinking we should create a simple python script, in the `scripts` folder, that runs all the scripts sequentially and records wall clock time and other metrics for each algorithm script in the system. Will this be a good idea, or will it been seen as something that is not necessary or not exactly aligned with the `no-magic` repo ideologies?
Cool. The script should be able to taken in parameters to either run the entire suite, specific section or specific list of scripts.
user_feedback_text: Cool. The script should be able to taken in parameters to either run the entire suite, specific section or specific list of scripts.
